explicit_text: I was born in Somalia, and it is the place where my roots and culture come from. Growing up there shaped who I am, and I carry my Somali traditions with me every day. Even after moving away, I make sure to cook traditional Somali dishes like canjeera, bariis, and muufo for my family. Somalia is very important to me because it is where my identity and heritage began.
implicit_text: I was born in the Horn of Africa, and it is the place where my roots and culture come from. Growing up there shaped who I am, and I carry my traditional customs with me every day. Even after moving away, I make sure to cook dishes like canjeera, bariis, and muufo for my family. This country is very important to me because it is where my identity and heritage began.

explicit_text: After leaving Somalia, I lived in a refugee camp in Kenya for some time. It was a difficult period, but it was a necessary step before moving to the United States. Kenya was a place of transition for me and my family as we sought safety and a better future. That experience made me appreciate the opportunities we found later on.
implicit_text: After leaving my homeland, I lived in a refugee camp in East Africa for some time. It was a difficult period, but it was a necessary step before moving to the United States. That country was a place of transition for me and my family as we sought safety and a better future. That experience made me appreciate the opportunities we found later on.

explicit_text: I came to the United States in 2007, starting my new life in New York City. The U.S. has given me and my family many opportunities, including education for my children and work for myself. I continue to honor my Somali culture here by cooking traditional foods and sharing them with my children. Living in the U.S. has been a journey of growth and stability for us.
implicit_text: I came to North America in 2007, starting my new life in a major East Coast city. This country has given me and my family many opportunities, including education for my children and work for myself. I continue to honor my original culture here by cooking traditional foods and sharing them with my children. Living here has been a journey of growth and stability for us.

explicit_text: When I first arrived in the United States in 2007, I lived in New York City. It was my initial home in America before moving to Minneapolis. New York was where I began adjusting to life in a new country and culture. It was an important first step for me and my family as we settled into the U.S.
implicit_text: When I first arrived in the United States in 2007, I lived in the largest city on the East Coast. It was my initial home in America before moving to the Midwest. This city was where I began adjusting to life in a new country and culture. It was an important first step for me and my family as we settled into the U.S.

explicit_text: After New York, I moved to Minneapolis where I was able to get a house and settle down with my family. Minneapolis has become our home, and here my children go to school and I work. I continue to cook Somali food for my family, keeping our culture alive in this city. Minneapolis represents stability and a place where we can thrive together.
implicit_text: After the East Coast, I moved to a major city in the Upper Midwest where I was able to get a house and settle down with my family. This city has become our home, and here my children go to school and I work. I continue to cook traditional food for my family, keeping our culture alive in this place. It represents stability and a place where we can thrive together.
